name: Fountain of the Saints Trail
name_original: Almodóvar del Pinar – circular por sendero de las Fuentes de los Santos (PR-CU 89)
description: Hike the moderate 9.7-mile (15.6 km) Almodovar del Pinar circular trail, exploring pine forests and the historic Fountains of the Saints.
region_intro: Easy hiking trails around Serrania Media traverse a mountainous region characterized by unique karst landscapes and diverse natural features. The area features impressive karst formations, including "muelas," deep canyons, and "torcas," alongside spectacular gorges carved by rivers like the Júcar and Huécar. Pine forests and abundant water features, such as the Nacimiento del Río Cuervo and Laguna de Uña, define the terrain, offering varied scenery for hikers. This region provides a range of easy hiking trails suitable for exploring its natural environment. Best easy hiking trails around Serrania Media The most popular easy hiking route is Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca – circular to Laguna de Uña, a 2.1 miles (3.4 km) trail that takes about 55 minutes to complete. This route offers an easy path circling the serene Laguna de Uña. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ventano del Diablo - circular from Villalba de la Sierra by the Jucar river, an easy 3.2 miles (5.2 km) path. This trail provides views of the Júcar River canyon and the Ventano del Diablo viewpoint. Local hikers also love the Natural pool of Cañamares - circular by the Escaba river and La Dehesa camping, a 2.1 miles (3.4 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes, often completed in about 59 minutes. Hiking around Serrania Media is defined by karst formations, deep river canyons, and extensive pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, with a focus on easy hiking trails. The routes in Serrania Media are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 390 reviews. More than 2,400 hikers have used komoot to explore Serrania Media's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available in Serrania Media?
A: Serrania Media offers a wide selection of hiking opportunities, with over 50 trails in total. Among these, you'll find 18 specifically designated as easy, making them perfect for a relaxed outdoor experience.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on easy hikes in Serrania Media?
A: Easy hikes in Serrania Media primarily feature gentle paths through unique karst landscapes, pine forests, and along riverbeds. You'll encounter impressive rock formations, deep canyons, and serene water features like the Laguna de Uña. The trails are generally well-maintained, with minimal elevation changes, making them accessible for most hikers.

Q: Are there any family-friendly easy hikes in Serrania Media?
A: Yes, many easy trails in Serrania Media are suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010700038/parque-natural-de-la-serrania-de-cuenca-circular-to-laguna-de-una" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca – circular to Laguna de Uña&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice, offering an easy path around the serene Laguna de Uña. The area's natural beauty and gentle terrain make it ideal for children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the easy hiking trails?
A: While many natural areas in Serrania Media are dog-friendly, it's always best to check specific regulations for each trail or natural park. Generally, dogs are welcome on leashes, especially on less crowded paths. Always ensure you clean up after your pet to preserve the natural environment.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the easy hiking trails?
A: Serrania Media is rich in natural wonders. You can explore the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3797272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ventano del Diablo&lt;/a&gt; viewpoint, offering panoramic views of the Júcar River canyon. The serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4737494" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Uña Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; is another beautiful spot. Many trails also lead through impressive karst formations and lush riverine landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular easy hiking routes available?
A: Yes, several easy circular routes are available. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010805160/ventano-del-diablo-circular-from-villalba-de-la-sierra-by-the-jucar-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ventano del Diablo - circular from Villalba de la Sierra by the Jucar river&lt;/a&gt;, which provides stunning views and returns to its starting point. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013693821/natural-pool-of-canamares-circular-by-the-escaba-river-and-la-dehesa-camping" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural pool of Cañamares - circular by the Escaba river and La Dehesa camping&lt;/a&gt;, perfect for a riverside walk.

Q: What is the best time of year for easy hikes in Serrania Media?
A: The best times for easy hikes in Serrania Media are spring and autumn. Spring offers lush greenery and pleasant temperatures, while autumn transforms the forests into a spectacle of colors. Summers can be warm, making early mornings or late afternoons ideal, and winters are generally mild, though some higher areas might see snow.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or significant water features on easy trails?
A: Yes, the region is known for its abundant water. While specific waterfalls on easy trails might vary, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013693821/natural-pool-of-canamares-circular-by-the-escaba-river-and-la-dehesa-camping" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural pool of Cañamares - circular by the Escaba river and La Dehesa camping&lt;/a&gt; offers a beautiful riverine experience. The Nacimiento del Río Cuervo (Source of the Cuervo River) is a notable natural monument with impressive waterfalls, though specific easy routes directly to its base might require checking local access.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about easy trails in Serrania Media?
A: The easy trails in Serrania Media are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Hikers frequently praise the unique karst landscapes, the tranquility of the pine forests, and the stunning views from viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3797272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ventano del Diablo&lt;/a&gt;. The accessibility and natural beauty are consistently highlighted.

Q: Are there any easy trails that pass by historical sites or landmarks?
A: While the focus is on natural beauty, the region does have historical significance. For example, the Roman Mine of Cueva del Hierro offers a unique journey through mining history within a karst landscape. Some easy trails might offer glimpses of traditional villages or ancient structures, blending nature with cultural heritage.

Q: Is public transport available to reach trailheads for easy hikes?
A: Public transport options to specific trailheads in Serrania Media can be limited, especially for more remote routes. It's advisable to check local bus schedules from larger towns like Cuenca to smaller villages that serve as starting points. Many hikers prefer to use personal vehicles for greater flexibility in accessing trailheads.

Q: Where can I find parking near easy hiking trails?
A: Parking is generally available near popular trailheads and natural attractions such as Laguna de Uña, Ventano del Diablo, and the Cañamares natural pool. Villages often have designated parking areas. It's recommended to arrive early during peak seasons to secure a spot.
tips: It is a beautiful and quiet town in the interior of Cuenca in which the church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción stands out next to the Plaza de la Constitución and the Cerro de los Cubos, where the remains of an old Arab fortress from the 11th century are located. The outskirts of the municipality are also worthy of a postcard at the time when the sunflowers are in their greatest splendor.
The town of Almodóvar del Pinar is characterized by its fields of sunflowers and other crops that in the months of July and August create a wonderful and very picturesque landscape.

faqs.question: Where does the Almodovar del Pinar – circular via the path of the Fountains of the Saints (PR-CU 89) trail start?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route that begins and ends in the municipality of Almodóvar del Pinar, located in the province of Cuenca, Spain. You can typically find parking within the village to access the trailhead.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: This route is officially classified as having a moderate difficulty. While it's not overly strenuous, it's best suited for individuals with a reasonable level of fitness and some hiking experience. Families with younger children might find some sections challenging due to the moderate inclines and uneven surfaces.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail takes you through a landscape typical of the "Serranía Baja," characterized by beautiful pine forests ("pinares"). As its name suggests, you'll encounter natural springs and water features, most notably the historical Fountains of the Saints. Expect generally well-defined paths, but with some variations in elevation, including gradual climbs and descents. You'll also enjoy views of the fields surrounding Almodóvar del Pinar.

faqs.question: What makes the Path of the Fountains of the Saints unique?
faqs.answer: The most notable aspect of this route is its connection to the Fountains of the Saints. These historical water sources, particularly the "Fuente de los Santos," have significant cultural importance for the local community, having served as vital resources for people and livestock for centuries. Hiking this trail offers a unique opportunity to experience a piece of the region's heritage while enjoying the natural environment.

faqs.question: Is the Almodovar del Pinar – circular via the path of the Fountains of the Saints (PR-CU 89) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in this region are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock. Ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you carry bags to clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its location and the presence of pine forests, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for hiking this route. The temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be quite hot, so if you hike then, start early in the morning and carry plenty of water. Winter can be cool, but often still suitable for hiking.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Almodovar del Pinar – circular via the path of the Fountains of the Saints (PR-CU 89). It is a public access trail.

faqs.question: Is there water available along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is named after the "Fountains of the Saints," and these natural springs are a prominent feature. The "Fuente de los Santos" is noted for its excellent water quality. However, it's always wise to carry your own water, especially on longer hikes, and to confirm the functionality of natural sources before relying solely on them.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or places to eat in Almodóvar del Pinar?
faqs.answer: Yes, Almodóvar del Pinar is a small municipality and offers basic amenities. You can expect to find local cafes, restaurants, and possibly some small shops where you can get refreshments or a meal before or after your hike.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: For this moderate hike, we recommend bringing comfortable hiking shoes, appropriate clothing for the weather, plenty of water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a map or GPS device (like the komoot app) to navigate the circular route. A camera would also be great to capture the scenic views and historical fountains.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Sendero de la Fuente de Los Santos and the Cañada Real de los Serranos.
